title: Worker enqueue trigger: auto-enqueue jobs on TurnCompleted [REOPENED - falsely closed]
description:
FALSELY CLOSED 2026-03-01. Worker receiver/queue/executor/result-handler was built, but NO CODE ever sends Action::WorkerEnqueue. The trigger — auto-enqueue on TurnCompleted — was never implemented. Zero worker jobs have ever run in production (14,659 signals, 30,498 events, 0 jobs).

WHAT EXISTS (dirty working tree, never committed):
- daemon.rs: worker_tx/worker_rx channels created
- daemon.rs: run() loop selects on worker_rx
- daemon.rs: handle_worker_job() dispatches to executor
- daemon.rs: apply_worker_result() routes results to FocusStateDelta + signals
- executor.rs: 5 job types implemented (classify_turn, extract_ascc_delta, detect_repetition, scan_for_errors, suggest_memory)
- types.rs: Action::WorkerEnqueue variant defined
- daemon.rs: translate_action handles WorkerEnqueue → forwards to worker_tx

WHAT IS MISSING:
- No code path constructs Action::WorkerEnqueue and sends it to command_tx
- After TurnCompleted: should enqueue ExtractAsccDelta + DetectRepetition + ScanForErrors
- After user input: should enqueue ClassifyTurn
- Periodically: should enqueue SuggestMemory
- detect_ufi_signals() exists but is never called from any code path

SPEC REFS: G1-10-workers, G1-detail-03-runtime-daemon §Background Scheduling, 44-pi-focusa-integration-spec §7

ACCEPTANCE:
- After TurnCompleted event, ExtractAsccDelta worker runs and populates FocusState slots (intent, decisions, constraints, failures, next_steps)
- After TurnCompleted, ScanForErrors worker runs and emits error signals to gate
- After TurnCompleted, DetectRepetition worker runs
- Worker results appear in focusa status/events
- FocusState on active frame is non-empty after proxied turns

ALSO: All 107 dirty files from this and related beads need to be committed to git.
notes:
Implemented worker queue handling in daemon: WorkerEnqueue now enqueues to worker_tx and emits WorkerJobEnqueued/Failed events; daemon run loop processes worker_rx and executes jobs via workers::executor, emitting WorkerJobStarted/Completed events and applying advisory results (ASCC delta update + signals). Added WorkerJob* FocusaEvent variants + reducer handling. Implemented project-scoped procedural rule filtering using active frame tags (project:<id>) and updated select_for_prompt signature + call sites. Added RuleRecord tags field (serde default).
AUDIT 2026-03-30: FALSELY CLOSED. Worker infrastructure (receiver/queue/executor/result handler) was built but NO CODE ever sends Action::WorkerEnqueue. The trigger side — auto-enqueue on TurnCompleted — was never implemented. Zero worker jobs have ever run. Additionally: all changes from this bead are in dirty working tree (107 files, 3977 insertions) — never committed to git.

title: Focusa cognitive loop: spec-faithful wiring [AUDIT 2026-03-30]
description:
Audit on 2026-03-30 found Focusa core is ~45% spec-faithful. Architecture, types, reducer are correct. But the cognitive loop — the thing that makes Focusa think — has two severed tendons:

1. SIGNAL → CANDIDATE PIPELINE: 14,659 signals accumulated, 0 candidates created. aggregate_signals() exists but is never called. The 5-step Focus Gate algorithm (G1-detail-06) is not wired into the daemon loop.

2. TURN → WORKER TRIGGER: Worker infrastructure exists (receiver, queue, executor, result handler) but nothing ever sends Action::WorkerEnqueue. Background cognition plane is dormant.

Downstream effects: Focus State on all frames is empty (all 10 ASCC slots blank). Expression Engine assembles prompts with no cognitive context. Autonomy scoring has 0 observations. ASCC checkpoints never created. UFI signals never detected.

This epic tracks all missing wiring to bring Focusa core to spec-faithful operation.

SUBSYSTEM FIDELITY SCORES (pre-fix):
- Reducer: 95% | Focus Stack: 80% | Focus Gate: 20% | Intuition Engine: 40%
- Focus State/apply_delta: 90% | ASCC Checkpointing: 5% | Expression Engine: 85%
- Proxy Adapter: 60% | Reference Store: 75% | Memory: 50%
- Autonomy: 10% | Workers: 10% | Event Persistence: 90%

CHILD BEADS: focusa-90r (reopened) + new beads below.

title: Signal → Candidate pipeline: wire aggregate_signals into daemon loop
description:
G1-detail-06 "Focus Gate Algorithm (MVP Deterministic Heuristics)" specifies a 5-step pipeline that is not wired into the daemon event loop.

SPEC REQUIREMENTS (exact):

Step 1: Normalize signals — On ingest: if payload_ref missing and payload is large -> store to ECS -> set payload_ref. Derive tags (error class, file path hints, tool name hints). Create fingerprint for dedupe: hash(kind + normalized summary + frame_context + key tags).

Step 2: Candidate matching or creation — If fingerprint matches existing candidate: increment times_seen, update last_seen_at, increase pressure by Δp. Else: create new candidate with base pressure.

Step 3: Pressure update rules — Additive base increments: user_input +0.6, tool_output +0.5, assistant_output +0.2, warning +0.7, error +1.2, repeated_pattern +0.8, manual_pin +2.0. Modifiers: goal alignment (related_frame == active: ×1.3, in stack path: ×1.1, else ×0.8), recency (within last 5 min: +0.3), risk (error/warning: +0.4). Suppression: if suppressed_until in future, do not surface. Decay: periodic tick pressure *= 0.98 for non-manual candidates.

Step 4: Surfacing — Candidates surfaced when pressure >= SURFACE_THRESHOLD (default 2.2) AND not suppressed AND not resolved. Surfacing emits gate.candidate_surfaced event. Returns candidate in API/UI lists.

Step 5: User actions — suppress, resolve, or convert to frame push.

CODE STATUS:
- base_pressure() matches spec values — EXISTS
- decay_candidates() with 0.98 factor, skips pinned — EXISTS
- surfaced_candidates() threshold filter — EXISTS
- aggregate_signals() groups by (kind, frame_context), calculates cumulative_pressure — EXISTS, ZERO CALL SITES
- suggest_candidate_kind() maps groups to CandidateKind — EXISTS, ZERO CALL SITES
- Steps 1-4 as active pipeline in daemon loop — MISSING
- Fingerprint dedup — MISSING
- Pressure modifiers (goal alignment ×1.3/×1.1/×0.8, recency +0.3, risk +0.4) — MISSING
- Signal Vec unbounded (14,659 signals, no cap) — spec says "Keep last N candidates (default 200)" for candidates; signals need equivalent bound

PERSISTENCE (spec): "Persist candidate list with bounded size. Keep last N candidates (default 200). Persist to ~/.focusa/state/focus_gate.json"

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Duplicate signals merge into same candidate
- Pressure increases predictably with repeated occurrences
- Suppression hides candidate for the set period
- Decay reduces stale candidates over time
- No candidate action changes focus stack without explicit CLI/API call
notes: Implemented run_gate_pipeline() with full 5-step algorithm. 7 new tests (14 total gate tests), 113 total pass. Signal cap 1000, candidate cap 200. Committed: 731a21c.

title: UXP/UFI: wire detect_ufi_signals into turn lifecycle
description:
docs/14-uxp-ufi-schema defines UFI (User Friction Index) with 14 signal types in 3 weight tiers:

High tier (objective): TaskReopened, ManualOverride, ImmediateCorrection, UndoOrRevert, ExplicitRejection.
Medium tier: Rephrase, RepeatRequest, ScopeClarification, ForcedSimplification.
Low tier (language-only — NEVER dominate aggregate): NegationLanguage, MetaLanguage, ImpatienceMarker, FrustrationIndicator, EscalationEvent.

CODE STATUS:
- UfiSignalType enum: all 14 types — EXISTS
- UfiWeightTier enum: High/Medium/Low — EXISTS
- UfiState with signals Vec and aggregate f64 — EXISTS
- detect_ufi_signals() in workers/executor.rs: detects UndoOrRevert, ExplicitRejection, ManualOverride, Rephrase, RepeatRequest, ScopeClarification, NegationLanguage — EXISTS, WORKS
- UxpProfile with 7 dimensions (autonomy_tolerance, verbosity_preference, interruption_sensitivity, explanation_depth, confirmation_preference, risk_tolerance, review_cadence) — EXISTS at defaults (all 0.5, confidence 0.0)

BUT: detect_ufi_signals() has ZERO call sites outside its own test. Nobody calls it on user input during turn lifecycle. UfiState.signals is always empty. UxpProfile never updated.

The spec states UXP dimensions use "α ≤ 0.1, window ≥ 30" for slow-moving calibration.

ACCEPTANCE:
- User input on each turn is passed through detect_ufi_signals()
- Detected UFI signals recorded in UfiState.signals
- UFI aggregate updated per turn
- UXP dimensions updated from UFI signals using EMA with α ≤ 0.1

title: Commit 107 dirty working-tree files to git
description:
git status shows 107 modified files and 17 untracked files totaling 3,977 insertions and 1,440 deletions — NEVER COMMITTED.

The running binary (target/release/focusa-daemon, built 2026-03-01 18:33) was compiled from this dirty tree, so production runs code that is not version-controlled.

Changes include:
- Worker infrastructure (queue, runner, executor integration) from focusa-90r
- Error envelope middleware, correlation IDs from focusa-d5g
- Reflection loop (API, CLI, persistence, scheduler) from focusa-ejo + focusa-2q4
- Capabilities API (commands, permissions, capabilities_extra) from focusa-1a7
- Kimi/Anthropic sanitization from focusa-97g
- Proxy transparency from focusa-m9i
- Various spec compliance fixes, docs updates, CLI additions

A git checkout main followed by cargo build would produce a daemon WITHOUT:
- Worker forwarding (back to TODO comment)
- Intuition engine TurnCompleted observation
- Capabilities API
- Reflection loop
- Error envelope middleware
- Turn idempotency
- Many CLI commands and API routes

This must be committed before any further development to prevent silent regression.

ACCEPTANCE:
- git status shows clean working tree
- git log shows atomic commits per logical change (not one giant commit)
- cargo build from clean checkout produces equivalent binary to current production
close_reason: 11 atomic commits: types+reducer, daemon+workers, core subsystems, adapters, middleware, API routes, CLI, TUI, docs+fixtures, build+CI, beads. git status clean. 107 modified + 24 untracked files → version-controlled.

title: Focus State population: enrich FocusStateDelta from turn content
description:
G1-07-ascc "Update Pipeline (MVP)" specifies that when a new turn arrives, delta content is summarized into structured slots and merged into existing checkpoint. This pipeline is not implemented.

SPEC REQUIREMENTS (exact):

Update Pipeline inputs: frame_id, turn_id, raw_user_input (small), assistant_output (small or handle), tool_outputs (handles), events relevant to this frame, optionally extracted facts/preferences from worker.

Delta Summarization Rule: 1) Determine delta content = only new items since last anchor. 2) Summarize delta into structured slots. 3) Merge into existing checkpoint using deterministic merge rules.

MVP summarization mechanism: "can be LLM-assisted OR rule-based. MVP can start with rule-based extraction (regex for file paths/errors) plus optional call to a cheap summarizer model if configured (NOT required)."

Spec requires: "You must implement a pluggable summarizer interface: Summarizer::summarize_delta(existing_checkpoint, delta_input) -> delta_sections"

Merge Rules are implemented in focus/state.rs apply_delta() — CORRECT. But apply_delta() is only ever called with current_state = token count string. All other slots permanently empty.

Specific merge rule gap — open_questions: spec says "if question is answered in delta, remove it (simple match heuristic)". Code only appends, never removes answered questions.

ASCC must also update: revision += 1, anchor_turn_id = turn_id, updated_at = now. Emit event: ascc.delta_applied.

CODE STATUS:
- FocusState struct: 10 slots matching spec — EXISTS
- FocusStateDelta struct: all 10 optional fields — EXISTS
- apply_delta() with caps (30/50/30/20/15/10/20/20): dedup, append, replace, prepend — EXISTS, CORRECT
- extract_ascc_delta() worker: heuristic extraction of decisions/next_steps/constraints/failures — EXISTS, NEVER CALLED
- Proxy FocusStateDelta on TurnCompleted: only sets current_state to "Turn <id>: X prompt + Y completion tokens" — INSUFFICIENT
- Pluggable Summarizer interface — MISSING
- open_questions remove-when-answered heuristic — MISSING
- ascc.delta_applied event emission — MISSING (FocusStateUpdated is emitted but not ascc.delta_applied)

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Delta updates never overwrite unrelated slots
- Anchors advance monotonically
- Prompt serialization stable and bounded
- Large tool outputs become handles (never inline)
notes: Implemented: broadened extract_ascc_delta (10 slots), proxy delta enrichment (both paths), open_questions remove-when-answered, apply_worker_result 8 fields. 114 tests pass. Committed: 2df5a8d.

title: ASCC CheckpointRecord lifecycle: instantiation, revision tracking, persistence
description:
G1-07-ascc "Structured Checkpoint Schema (MVP)" defines CheckpointRecord with fields: frame_id, revision (u64), updated_at, anchor_turn_id (last processed turn), sections (AsccSections), breadcrumbs (Vec<HandleRef>), confidence (optional, MVP can omit), history (Vec<AsccDeltaMeta>, bounded, optional).

The spec states: "Checkpoint per frame stored in: ~/.focusa/ascc/<frame_id>.json. MVP: only current checkpoint required."

The spec states ASCC provides two serializers: to_string_compact() and to_messages_slots() for prompt assembly.

The UPDATE section adds per-slot metadata: pinned (bool), last_updated_at. And ASCC must expose to_digest() for ultra-compact fallback when prompt budget cannot be satisfied.

CODE STATUS:
- CheckpointRecord struct in types.rs — EXISTS, NEVER INSTANTIATED (zero usage outside type definition)
- AsccSections struct in types.rs — EXISTS, NEVER INSTANTIATED
- ascc_checkpoint_id on FrameRecord — always None, never set
- ~/.focusa/ascc/ directory — DOES NOT EXIST
- to_string_compact() — MISSING
- to_messages_slots() — MISSING
- to_digest() — MISSING (build_focus_digest in expression engine is a local workaround, not the spec interface)
- Per-slot pinned/last_updated_at metadata — MISSING
- Pluggable Summarizer interface — MISSING

FocusState on FrameRecord is being used as a de-facto ASCC substitute (same 10 slots), but lacks: revision tracking, anchor_turn_id, breadcrumbs, history, serializers, per-slot pinning, persistence as separate file.

SPEC INVARIANTS:
- "ASCC degradation is explicit"
- "ASCC never silently truncates pinned sections"

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Delta updates never overwrite unrelated slots
- Anchors advance monotonically
- Prompt serialization stable and bounded
- Large tool outputs become handles (never inline)
notes: Implemented: ascc.rs module with CheckpointRecord lifecycle (from_frame, update_from_frame, revision tracking), FocusState→AsccSections conversion, 3 serializers (to_string_compact, to_messages_slots, to_digest), daemon checkpoint management (create/update on FocusStateUpdated), all 3 prompt assembly callers now pass AsccSections. 122 tests pass. Committed: fd42b42.

title: FrameRecord: update FrameStats on turns, store completion_reason and completed_at
description:
G1-detail-05 defines FrameStats (MVP minimal): turn_count (u64), last_turn_id (Option<String>), last_token_estimate (Option<u32>). These must be updated as turns flow through the proxy.

G1-detail-05 UPDATE "Frame Completion Semantics" adds to FrameRecord: completed_at (Option<timestamp>), completion_reason (Option<FrameCompletionReason>). The UPDATE states: "Completion reason MUST be reflected in: recent_results, or failures (if error/blocked)." And: "Completion reason contributes signals: blocked → raises surface pressure on related candidates. abandoned → suppress related candidates."

CODE STATUS:
- FrameStats struct: EXISTS with correct fields
- turn_count: always 0, never incremented (reducer FocusFramePushed sets stats: FrameStats::default(), never updated on TurnCompleted)
- last_turn_id: always None
- last_token_estimate: always None
- completed_at: NOT A FIELD on FrameRecord (spec UPDATE requires it)
- completion_reason: exists as FocusaEvent::FocusFrameCompleted field but NOT stored on FrameRecord after completion (reducer sets status to Completed but discards the reason)
- Completion reason → Focus Gate signals (blocked raises pressure, abandoned suppresses) — NOT IMPLEMENTED

ACCEPTANCE:
- After proxied turns, active frame FrameStats.turn_count > 0
- last_turn_id reflects most recent turn
- last_token_estimate reflects assembled prompt size
- On frame complete, completed_at and completion_reason persisted on FrameRecord
- Completion with reason=blocked emits signal raising pressure on related gate candidates
- Completion with reason=abandoned suppresses related gate candidates

title: Autonomy scoring: wire record_observation into turn lifecycle
description:
docs/12-autonomy-scoring "Data Sources (All Verifiable)" states: "All autonomy scoring derives from existing Focusa data: Primary Sources — Reducer event log, Focus Stack transitions, Focus State updates, Reference Store usage, Beads task lifecycle events."

docs/12 "Scoring Categories & Weights (MVP)": Outcome 50%, Efficiency 20%, Discipline 15%, Safety 15%.

docs/12 "Confidence & Sample Size": "ARI is always accompanied by sample size, confidence band. Low sample size reduces promotion eligibility, not ARI itself."

docs/37-autonomy-calibration-spec defines 6 ARI dimensions: Correctness, Stability, Efficiency, Trust, Grounding, Recovery. And specifies EMA update with alpha.

CODE STATUS:
- compute_ari() — EXISTS, correct weighted formula
- update_dimension() — EXISTS, EMA with alpha
- should_recommend_promotion() — EXISTS, threshold ladder (AL0→AL1 at ARI>=40 samples>=10, etc.)
- record_observation() — EXISTS, updates all 6 dimensions, increments sample_count, recomputes ARI
- grant_level() — EXISTS, records history event

BUT: record_observation() has ZERO call sites outside autonomy/mod.rs. Nobody in daemon, API routes, or proxy ever calls it. Production state: ARI=0.0, samples=0, all dimensions=0.0.

No code derives observation scores from turn events (e.g., task completion → correctness, focus thrashing → stability, token usage → efficiency, scope violations → trust).

ACCEPTANCE:
- After turn lifecycle events, autonomy dimensions update via record_observation()
- ARI score reflects weighted aggregate
- sample_count increments with each observation
- focusa autonomy status shows non-zero ARI after usage
- Promotion recommendations appear when ARI and samples meet threshold ladder

title: ECS auto-externalization: enforce threshold on turn content
description:
G1-detail-08 "Threshold Policy (MVP)" states: "Config: ecs.externalize_bytes_threshold default 8KB, ecs.externalize_token_estimate_threshold default 800 tokens. If either exceeded, externalize."

G1-detail-11 "Prompt Inclusion Policy" states: "In prompts, include handles only. If model must see the content, provide an explicit retrieval step."

44-pi-focusa-integration-spec §7.4 "ECS Offload Rules": "Externalize when content exceeds either threshold: bytes > 8KB, or estimated tokens > 800. Prompt includes only handles: [HANDLE:<kind>:<id> \"label\"]"

CODE STATUS:
- FocusaConfig has ecs_externalize_bytes_threshold (8192) and ecs_externalize_token_threshold (800) — EXISTS
- ReferenceStore.store() works correctly — EXISTS
- Proxy routes (openai + anthropic) receive assistant_output but NEVER check size against threshold
- turn.rs turn_complete receives assistant_output but NEVER checks size against threshold
- No code path auto-externalizes large turn content to ECS and replaces with handle
- Large assistant outputs are stored as raw strings in TurnCompleted events (some >8KB)

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Storing same content results in distinct handles but same sha
- Resolving returns exact bytes written
- Large blobs never appear inline in prompt assembly
- Tool outputs and assistant responses exceeding 8KB or 800 tokens are auto-externalized to ECS
- TurnCompleted event carries HandleRef instead of raw content for large outputs

title: Intuition Engine: implement missing signal sources (consistency, repeated edits/tools)
description:
docs/05-intuition-engine "Signal Sources (MVP)" defines 4 categories:

1. Temporal Signals: "Frame duration exceeds expected bounds. Prolonged inactivity." — IMPLEMENTED (observe_stack checks frame duration > 30min, observe_turn checks inactivity > 5min)

2. Repetition Signals: "Repeated errors. Repeated edits. Repeated tool invocations." — PARTIAL (error string matching exists: "error", "Error", "panic", "failed". Repeated edits NOT detected. Repeated tool invocations NOT detected.)

3. Consistency Signals: "Contradictory decisions. Drift between stated intent and actions." — NOT IMPLEMENTED

4. Structural Signals: "Deep stack nesting. Frequent frame switching." — PARTIAL (deep nesting > 3 frames detected. Frequent frame switching NOT detected.)

Also from docs/05: "Aggregation: Signals are aggregated by type, related frame, time window. Aggregation produces: cumulative pressure, summarized description." — EXISTS in aggregation.rs but never called (covered by focusa-syh.1).

CODE STATUS:
- IntuitionEngine.observe_turn(): only detects errors by substring match — PARTIAL
- IntuitionEngine.observe_stack(): detects deep nesting + long-running frames — CORRECT
- detect_error_repetition(): fires at threshold (3 errors, every 3rd after) — EXISTS
- detect_inactivity(): fires after 5min gap — EXISTS
- No tracking of edits or tool invocations
- No contradictory decision detection
- No frequent frame switching detection

SPEC INVARIANTS (exact):
- "Runs asynchronously only"
- "Cannot block the hot path"
- "Cannot mutate Focus State or Focus Stack"
- "Emits signals, not commands"
- "All signals are explainable"

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Signals reflect real patterns
- No noise storms
- No runaway pressure
- Fully explainable output

title: Expression Engine: pass ASCC sections and parent context to prompt assembly
description:
G1-detail-11 "Slot-Based Structure (Canonical)" defines 7 slots. Slot 3 (Active Focus Frame) spec: "Serialize ASCC slots for active frame." Slot 4 (Parent Context): "Include up to N ancestors (default 2). Rules: include only intent, decisions, constraints. Omit artifacts unless explicitly required."

G1-detail-05 "How HEC Influences Prompt Assembly": "Prompt assembly always includes: Active frame ASCC checkpoint slots. Optionally includes parent ASCC checkpoints (bounded by budget). Never includes siblings or unrelated frames in MVP. HEC provides focus proximity: active = 0, parent = 1, grandparent = 2. Used by prompt assemblers inclusion heuristics."

CODE STATUS:
- assemble_from() accepts AssemblyInput with ascc, parent_frames, rules, handles — EXISTS, CORRECT
- build_focus_slot() renders ASCC or FocusState — EXISTS, CORRECT
- build_parents_slot() renders up to MAX_PARENT_FRAMES (2) ancestors — EXISTS, CORRECT
- build_handles_slot() renders handle references — EXISTS, CORRECT

BUT all callers pass incomplete input:
- openai::process_request() passes ascc: None — always
- anthropic::process_request() passes ascc: None — always (inferred from same pattern)
- turn.rs prompt_assemble passes ascc: None — always
- All callers pass parent_frames: &[] — never builds ParentContext from stack

The engine is correct but starved. Depends on focusa-syh.3 (ASCC instantiation) and focusa-syh.2 (Focus State population).

G1-detail-11 also specifies "Delta Injection Rule": "Prompt Assembly must reuse prior assembled prompt context, inject only changes since last turn where possible." — NOT IMPLEMENTED (each turn builds full prompt from scratch).

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Prompt size stabilizes across turns
- No duplicated ASCC content
- Handles never inline by default
- Budget enforcement deterministic

title: Memory: emit events for mutations (semantic upsert, rule reinforce, decay)
description:
G1-detail-03 §Event Log: "every state mutation must emit an event." G1-detail-15 §Memory events: memory.semantic_upserted, memory.rule_reinforced, memory.decay_tick.

core-reducer.md §Canonical Event Types lists Memory actions: UpsertSemantic, ReinforceRule, DecayTick. These should go through the reducer as events.

CODE STATUS:
- daemon.rs translate_action for UpsertSemantic: directly calls memory::semantic::upsert() then persistence.save_state() — BYPASSES REDUCER, no event emitted
- daemon.rs translate_action for ReinforceRule: directly calls memory::procedural::reinforce() then persistence.save_state() — BYPASSES REDUCER, no event emitted
- daemon.rs translate_action for DecayTick: directly calls decay functions — BYPASSES REDUCER for memory (gate decay does go through state mutation but no event)
- No FocusaEvent variants for memory mutations (MemorySemanticUpserted, MemoryRuleReinforced, MemoryDecayTick not in FocusaEvent enum)
- core-reducer.md pseudocode lists these as reducer actions but types.rs FocusaEvent enum does not include them

This violates: "If a cognition change cannot be expressed as a reducer event, it does not belong in Focusa." (core-reducer.md §Final Canonical Rule)

Also from G1-detail-15 "Replay Invariant": "Focusa state MUST be reconstructible by replaying events in order." Memory mutations that bypass the event log break this invariant.

ACCEPTANCE:
- UpsertSemantic emits memory.semantic_upserted event through reducer
- ReinforceRule emits memory.rule_reinforced event through reducer
- DecayTick emits memory.decay_tick event through reducer
- All memory mutations appear in event log
- State is reconstructible by replaying events (replay invariant)

title: Spec gap: add capabilities read domains (/v1/agents, /v1/state/*, /v1/lineage/*, /v1/references/*)
description: Implement authoritative capabilities read endpoints from docs/23 with pagination/filters and policy guards.
notes: Implemented routes in crates/focusa-api/src/routes/capabilities.rs and wired into server/router. Added endpoints: /v1/agents(+/{id},/constitution,/capabilities), /v1/state/current|history|stack|diff, /v1/lineage/head|node/{id}|path/{id}|children/{id}|summaries, /v1/references. Validation: tests/build/restart + curl smoke.
close_reason: Completed: capabilities read domains implemented (/v1/agents*, /v1/state/*, /v1/lineage/*, /v1/references) with permission gating via x-focusa-permissions + default scopes; tests/build/restart + curl smoke pass.

title: Tests: scheduler cadence + cap/cooldown compliance
description: Add API/CLI tests for scheduler config, cadence trigger behavior, and cap/cooldown enforcement; extend probe if needed.
acceptance_criteria: cargo tests + API contract probe pass with scheduler guardrail checks.
close_reason: Completed: added reflection scheduler tests in routes/reflection.rs and extended scripts/api_contract_probe.py with scheduler contract checks; full tests + probe green.

title: Core: enforce per-window iteration caps + cooldown ledger
description: Implement durable window/cooldown counters used by reflection scheduler + manual runs to enforce max-iterations and cooldown policy.
acceptance_criteria: Per-window cap and cooldown are enforced deterministically across restarts.
close_reason: Completed: implemented durable guardrail ledger tables reflection_window_counters + scheduler config persistence; enforced cap/cooldown checks in scheduler-triggered reflection execution.
